sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Inspect g and G node positions when Middle is selected
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(r for r, c in b)
        max_row = max(r for r, c in b)
        min_col = min(c for r, c in b)
        max_col = max(c for r, c in b)
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min_row},{min_col})-({max_row},{max_col})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the pattern more clearly:
When Middle is selected:
- G frame: (17,22)-(22,27) - around Middle cross
- g markers: (18,12)-(18,13), (19,11)-(20,11), (19,14)-(20,14), (21,12)-(21,13)
- Left cross: (18,17)-(21,20)
The g markers are at cols 11-14, which is to the LEFT of the Left cross (which is at cols 17-20). So the g markers are on the LEFT side of Left cross, not between Middle and Left.
When Left was selected:
- G frame: (17,16)-(22,21) - around Left cross
- g markers: (18,30)-(18,31), (19,29)-(20,29), (19,32)-(20,32), (21,30)-(21,31)
- Middle cross: (18,23)-(21,26)
- Right cross: (18,35)-(21,38)
The g markers are at cols 29-32, which is between Middle (cols 23-26) and Right (cols 35-38).
So the pattern is:
- When Left is selected, g markers appear between Middle and Right
- When Middle is selected, g markers appear to the LEFT of Left
This is a very specific pattern. Let me think about what it means...
The g markers form a cross-like shape (4 pairs of pixels arranged like a cross). They're positioned:
- When Left selected: between Middle and Right (pointing to Right)
- When Middle selected: to the left of Left (pointing away from Middle)
Wait, maybe the g markers indicate the NEXT cross to click? When Left is selected, the g markers are between Middle and Right, pointing towards Right. But I already tried Left → Right and it reset!
Unless... the g markers indicate the NEXT cross AFTER the one I should click? Like, Left → Middle → Right? But I tried Left → Middle → Right and it reset too!
Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe the g markers are NOT indicating the next step, but rather indicating something about the relationship between crosses.
Wait, let me look at the positions more carefully:
- Left cross: cols 17-20
- Middle cross: cols 23-26
- Right cross: cols 35-38
- Lower-top cross: rows 24-27, cols 41-44
- Lower-bottom cross: rows 36-39, cols 41-44
When Left is selected, g markers are at cols 29-32 (between Middle and Right). The distance from Left to Middle is 3 columns (20 to 23), and from Middle to Right is 9 columns (26 to 35). The g markers are centered around col 30-31, which is roughly in the middle of the gap between Middle and Right.
When Middle is selected, g markers are at cols 11-14 (to the left of Left). The distance from Middle to Left is 3 columns (23 to 20). The g markers are centered around col 12-13, which is 4-5 columns to the left of Left.
Hmm, this doesn't seem to follow a simple pattern.
Let me think about this differently. What if the g markers are actually "arrows" pointing in a specific direction?
- When Left is selected: g markers are between Middle and Right, pointing RIGHT (towards Right cross)
- When Middle is selected: g markers are to the left of Left, pointing LEFT (away from the other crosses)
So the arrows are:
- Left → points RIGHT (towards Right cross, skipping Middle)
- Middle → points LEFT (away from everything)
This is confusing. Let me try a completely different approach.
What if the game is NOT about clicking on crosses, but about clicking on the g-marked cells? When I click on Left, g markers appear. What if I need to click on those g markers?
Let me try clicking on one of the g-marked cells when Left is selected.

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 18}])
</parameter>
